slides.title: 1. Fractures (Broken Bones)
slides.description:
A fracture is a crack or break in a bone.

  • Simple (Closed) Fracture: Bone breaks but stays inside the skin.
  • Compound (Open) Fracture: Bone breaks and tears through the skin.
  • Greenstick Fracture: Bone bends and cracks. Common in babies because their bones are soft.
  • Comminuted Fracture: Bone shatters into many pieces.

slides.title: 2. Sprains and Strains
slides.description:
Sprain: An injury to a LIGAMENT (torn or over-stretched). Occurs at joints.
Strain: An injury to a MUSCLE or Tendon (torn or over-stretched).

First Aid (R.I.C.E):
Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation.

slides.title: 3. Dislocation
slides.description:
Definition: When a bone is forced out of its normal position at a joint.

Signs: Severe pain, swelling, and the joint looks deformed.

First Aid: Do NOT try to force the bone back. Apply a cold compress and seek medical help immediately.

slides.title: 4. First Aid for Bones
slides.description:
Splints: Hard objects (wood, plastic) tied alongside a broken limb to keep the bone straight and prevent movement.

Arm Sling: A cloth tied around the neck to support a broken or injured arm.

Stretcher: Used to carry a victim who cannot walk (e.g., leg or spine injury).
